# v6.45.2 — Curator validation gate and curator_analyze entry_id hardening

Follow-up to v6.45.1, addressing two post-merge findings from code review of the hallucinated `entry_id` fix.

## What changed

### [HIGH] Auto-generated knowledge entries now pass through the validation gate

New `SwarmKnowledgeEntry` records created by the `promote new` path in
`applyCuratorKnowledgeUpdates` were previously appended directly to `knowledge.jsonl`
without consulting the repo's three-layer knowledge validation system. Every other
ingestion path (knowledge-add, knowledge-migrator, hive-promoter) calls `validateLesson()`
and respects `knowledge.validation_enabled`. This path now does too:

- Existing lessons are loaded before the append loop for dedup/contradiction checking.
- `validateLesson(lesson, existingLessons, { category, scope, confidence })` is called when
  `validation_enabled !== false`.
- Lessons that fail validation are skipped (counted in `skipped`, not `applied`).
- Each successfully appended lesson is added to `existingLessons` so subsequent entries in
  the same call can deduplicate against it.

### [MEDIUM] `curator_analyze` now rejects invalid `entry_id` values instead of silently coercing

The previous fix applied UUID v4 normalisation to both the LLM text-parse path and the
direct `curator_analyze` tool path. For the tool path, silently coercing a stale or
typo'd `entry_id` string to `undefined` caused unintended new-entry creation instead of
surfacing a caller error.

`curator_analyze` now validates each recommendation's `entry_id` before calling
`applyCuratorKnowledgeUpdates`:
- `undefined` is valid — creates a new entry.
- A valid UUID v4 is valid — updates the referenced entry.
- Any other string returns `{ "error": "Invalid entry_id '...': must be a UUID v4 or omitted." }`
  and writes nothing.

The UUID v4 normalisation (slug → `undefined`) is intentionally retained only in
`parseKnowledgeRecommendations`, which processes LLM text output where hallucinated slugs
are an expected occurrence.

## Tests added

- `curator.test.ts`: skips new entry when lesson fails validation gate (`validation_enabled=true`)
- `curator.test.ts`: creates entry when `validation_enabled=false` (bypass confirmed)
- `tests/unit/tools/curator-analyze.test.ts` (new file): 3 tests for entry_id validation —
  rejects non-UUID, accepts `undefined`, accepts valid UUID v4

## Migration steps

None — validation was already on by default. The only observable change is that
auto-generated curator lessons that would have failed the existing validation rules
(dangerous commands, injection patterns, etc.) are now silently skipped rather than persisted.

## Breaking changes

None for the LLM text-parse path. Direct callers of `curator_analyze` that previously
supplied non-UUID `entry_id` strings will now receive an error response instead of
having the value silently coerced. This is the correct behavior and was a bug in the
prior release.
